summary: No-ship. The two flip rows are valid witnesses of the same abort-to-completion behavior; record consistency, ancestry, scope, and test preservation check out. Mandatory release-profile, original-repro, and anti-cheat mutant evidence is absent, and one integration witness can pass without covering its claimed branch.

findings.title: Missing verification: release-profile controls
findings.body: The plan makes two `cargo test --release` commands mandatory and calls them the only control against profile-dependent behavior. The committed evidence shows the debug witness gate followed only by a `dev` build; it contains no release command or output. Production-profile divergence therefore remains untested.
findings.recommendation: Have the status script execute both exact release commands from plan lines 2197-2199 at green.sha and commit their raw output and exit status.

findings.title: Missing verification: original un-minimised repro
findings.body: The green script-owned record explicitly contains `repro: null`. The diagnosis says the original repro was 40 buffered puts racing reconciliation, but its characterization `ok` line is insufficient: the test neither proves a reconciliation iteration ran nor that the vanished-entry race occurred, and the red version passed while discarding reconciliation errors.
findings.recommendation: Add a script-owned original-repro command with a non-vacuous race/iteration witness, execute the documented workload at green.sha, and commit its raw output in the verify record and verification.md.

findings.title: Missing verification: gate-predicate mutation kills
findings.body: The plan requires individually removing every discovery/result predicate plus the SIGPIPE and old-parser controls and observing the selftest fail. Verification contains only the unmodified `SELFTEST: PASS` output, so it does not prove the gate cannot again false-green after one of its predicates is weakened.
findings.recommendation: Run the required final-tree predicate mutants, capture each expected RED result, and commit the raw mutation audit.

findings.title: Phase G does not prove the recovery remove branch was exercised
findings.body: After waiting, the test deletes every remaining grave itself and later defines `stepped_k` merely as an intact K blob with no grave. That condition is true whether production executed the remove branch or the test deleted the grave, so the claimed two-branch coverage assertion can pass without exercising a vanished K grave.
findings.recommendation: Assert the rendezvous was reached and count successful external deletions separately for R and K graves; require at least one K deletion before accepting remove-branch coverage.

next_steps: Repair the Phase G witness before refreshing evidence.
Regenerate machine-owned verification at green.sha with release-profile, original-repro, and gate-mutant outputs.
Do not merge until the refreshed committed evidence satisfies every acceptance requirement.
